description: This set of figures characterizes the microstructure and strain partitioning behavior of Zr0.50 zirconium-alloyed Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel with heterogeneous face-centered cubic (FCC) grain structure. Subfigure (a) is an EBSD image quality (IQ) map at 0% global strain, distinguishing fine FCC grain (FG) zones and coarse FCC grain (CG) zones with a 20 μm scale bar. Subfigures (b) and (c) are von Mises strain maps obtained via quasi in-situ μ-DIC at 2% and 8% global strain respectively, both with 20 μm scale bars and a strain range of 0 to 12. At 2% global strain, high strain concentrates in CG zones and FG/CG heterointerfaces, while FG zones remain nearly undeformed. When global strain increases to 8%, local strain in FG zones becomes pronounced, confirming persistent strain partitioning between soft CG and hard FG zones during plastic deformation. These results verify that strain partitioning induces geometrically necessary dislocations, enhancing strain hardening and enabling strength-ductility synergy of the heterogeneous steel.
